questions.title: Text placement opcodes (OP_04 / OP_05 / OP_06)
questions.scenario: You step on an outdoor arrow sign vs a building nameplate vs a long quest plaque.

options.A: OP_06 = framed signposts with arrows/rules; OP_05 = long borderless lore/warnings; OP_04 = door/shop labels only (never dungeon wall fluff).
options.B: OP_05 and OP_06 are interchangeable; only string length differs.
options.C: OP_04 is used for both doors and outdoor signs; OP_06 is dungeons only.
options.D: OP_05 is the default signpost; you have seen it with arrow glyphs where we expect OP_06.

questions.re_guess_detail: event.dat usage (~110 OP_06 sign strings vs ~40 OP_05 plaques; OP_04 clusters on town service tiles).
questions.example: loc 0 Middlegate: OP_04 "Middlegate Inn"; loc 4 Sandsobar: OP_05 "Beware of Eyes" plaque, OP_06 "The / Slums" sign; loc 14 C3: OP_06 "<Sandsobar / <Hillstone" arrow signpost.

questions.title: Basic vs block text (OP_01 / OP_02 / OP_03)
questions.scenario: Nordon/Nordonna intro dialogue vs a one-line "Travel (y/n)?" prompt vs a multi-paragraph NPC speech box.

options.A: OP_01 = single-line / simple prompts; OP_02 = multi-line scroll block; OP_03 = same block as OP_02 after extra window prep.
options.B: Nordon intro uses OP_01; all y/n gates use OP_02.
options.C: All three look identical on screen — opcode choice is arbitrary.
options.D: OP_03 is the only opcode that can show more than one paragraph.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 60 Nordon strings are referenced as blocks; town y/n tiles commonly decompile to OP_01 + prompt_yes_no.
questions.example: loc 0 Middlegate ev 21: OP_01 "Exit (y/n)?"; ev 34: OP_02 Otto cartography block; loc 60 Nordon prologue blocks; loc 57 Pinehurst: OP_03 time-distortion plaque.

questions.title: Engine thunks (OP_0D)
questions.scenario: A tile runs script bytes but never opens a shop window (OP_0E).

options.A: Never — OP_0D is always followed by visible text you remember.
options.B: Yes — e.g. cruise/arena return: engine_call(0x09) then map_transition, with no shop selector.
options.C: Yes — chest/treasure UI opens directly from OP_0D alone.
options.D: OP_0D only appears in overlay banks 60–70, not walkable maps.

questions.re_guess_detail: Multiple overworld scripts call engine_call(0x09) paired with map_transition (e.g. cruise ride loc 14, Corak's Cave door loc 22); per-index table still Unknown.
questions.example: loc 14 C3 tile (9,6): "Cruise to Murray's Resort Isle" → engine_call(0x09) → map_transition — no exec_selector. Also blizzard tiles loc 5/6 and Joan's travel booking loc 0.

questions.title: Service title window position (OP_0B arg2)
questions.scenario: Same inn/blacksmith tile, different party facing or roster size.

options.A: Title box always appears in the same screen slot; arg2 is a mode/id, not facing math.
options.B: Box shifts vertically based on party facing (like OP_05 popups).
options.C: Box shifts when you have more than four live members.
options.D: You have never noticed the service title box at all — only the shop UI.

questions.re_guess_detail: arg2 is passed to -$7FBC as a mode byte; no facing hook traced in the OP_0B handler (Partial).
questions.example: loc 0 Middlegate tile (14,7): set_service_context("Arena Entrance Only!", mode=0x00) then exec_selector. All town OP_0B tiles use mode=0x00.

questions.title: Unknown OP_0E selectors (default range bins)
questions.scenario: Arena entrance, Monster Bowl, guild enrollment beyond mage guild (0x0D).

options.A: Arena combat is not OP_0E — entirely separate combat trigger; selectors 0x09–0x10 are misc shops only.
options.B: Town arena tiles call unnamed selectors (e.g. Middlegate 0x10, Sandsobar 0x49) that enter combat/ticket flow inside the engine thunk.
options.C: Every selector 0x09–0xFB opens the same generic shop UI with different inventory tables only.
options.D: Arena entry uses selector 0x64 (portal travel).

questions.re_guess_detail: Arena entrance tiles decompile to exec_selector(0x10) / 0x49 / etc., not OP_12 in the visible script (Partial).
questions.example: Middlegate loc 0: exec_selector(0x10) on arena pit; Sandsobar loc 4: exec_selector(0x49); Vulcania: 0x3E–0x40. Contrast: blacksmith uses 0x06 open_blacksmith_shop.

questions.title: Special OP_0E selectors (0x64, 0xC9–0xCF, 0xE2, 0xFD)
questions.scenario: Mystic portal, quest milestones, endgame.

options.A: 0x64 = long-distance portal travel; 0xC9–0xCF = quest-specific handlers; 0xFD = late-game / unused in normal play.
options.B: All of these are just renamed blacksmith shops.
options.C: 0xE2 = arena only; 0x64 = boat ferry.
options.D: You fire 0xFD regularly in early game.

questions.re_guess_detail: Names in SELECTOR_NAMES + portal tiles using high selectors; 0xFD seen rarely (Partial).
questions.example: loc 10 B2 tile (12,12): check_day_of_year(140–170) then exec_selector(0x64) circus portal. Atlantium "The Mystic Portal" uses normal service chain.

questions.title: Encounter tail bytes (OP_12 / OP_13)
questions.scenario: Same monster IDs, different fight feel (arena backdrop, boss tone, no escape).

options.A: Tail byte 1 = terrain/backdrop id; tail byte 2 = flags (escape, arena, difficulty band).
options.B: Both bytes are fixed 0x00 in all real fights — purely padding.
options.C: Tail bytes set party level / scaled HP only.
options.D: Tail byte 1 = gold reward multiplier; byte 2 unused.

questions.re_guess_detail: Scripts emit varied non-zero tails (06 02, 11 5C, 05 F0, …) with stable 10-byte monster blocks (Partial, tail table not dumped).
questions.example: loc 8 D1 ev 10: flags=11 5C outdoor fight; loc 22 Corak's Cave: flags=1B 01 boss pack; loc 24 Ice Cavern: many varied tails (21 5A, 03 F0, 05 F0).

questions.title: Party byte apply (OP_15 / masked OP_18)
questions.scenario: Olympic Trial skill purchase, hire restrictions, quest progress bits.

options.A: Sets per-member flag/field bytes (skills owned, class gates, quest bits) — not direct HP damage.
options.B: Always deducts gold when OP_15 runs (same opcode as payment).
options.C: Only used for alignment/reputation changes you see in a stat panel.
options.D: You have never noticed any effect from these ops in play.

questions.re_guess_detail: Atlantium Olympic Trial uses apply_party / apply_party_masked on field 0x6D before party_effect skill grant (Partial).
questions.example: loc 1 Atlantium Olympic Trial ev 28: apply_party(0x6D) then apply_party_masked sets Athlete bit before party_effect teaches skill. Otto cartography uses same 0x6D pattern.

questions.title: Party effects (OP_1F / OP_20)
questions.scenario: Fountain heals, temple cure, trainer spell/skill grant, stat shrine.

options.A: Selector byte chooses effect class (heal, cure, teach skill/spell, buff); OP_20 is a variant mode of the same dispatcher.
options.B: Only restores HP — poison/cure uses a different opcode family entirely.
options.C: Purely cosmetic — no stat or HP change.
options.D: Always grants a random item.

questions.re_guess_detail: party_effect(sel=0x09, …) follows skill prompts; party_effect(sel=0x80, …) follows shrine/fountain chains (Partial).
questions.example: Olympic Trial: party_effect(sel=0x09) teaches Athlete; Murray's Gym loc 16: sel=0x80 workout; Sandsobar Cavern trainer uses party_effect_b (OP_20) per class.

questions.title: Map tile mutation (OP_21)
questions.scenario: Barrier drops, door opens, wall becomes floor — still there after leaving the tile.

options.A: Yes — e.g. Corak's Cave barriers lowered with visible map change (set_tile on adjacent cells).
options.B: Only clears the event bit (OP_14); graphics never change.
options.C: Visual change is session-only — resets on town exit.
options.D: You recall permanent changes but they were cut-scene transitions, not tile writes.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 22 Hero's Tomb script writes collision/visual bytes via OP_21 then OP_14 (Partial).
questions.example: loc 22 Corak's Cave ev 5: Murray opens Hero's Tomb — set_tile on (3,7)/(3,8) and (2,7)/(2,8) then clear_current_tile_event_flag.

questions.title: Non-gold predicate codes (OP_25 values 0, 1, 2)
questions.scenario: Our decoder labels flag_clear / flag_set / flag_alt.

options.A: Generic script/state flags (quest done, already paid, etc.) — not item IDs.
options.B: Directly encode ticket colors (0=green … 2=black).
options.C: Bishop freed / arena won / goblet returned map 1:1 to codes 0/1/2.
options.D: You have never hit a gate you would attribute to OP_25 — likely wrong decode.

questions.re_guess_detail: --predicates finds zero recoverable OP_25 hits in event.dat; small integer codes fit flag words, not items (Partial, high uncertainty).
questions.example: OP_25 appears zero times in decoded event.dat scripts — no recoverable in-game gate to cite. If you've never noticed one, RE may be wrong or flags live in engine RAM.

questions.title: Tickets/keys: check vs consume (OP_25 vs OP_28)
questions.scenario: Arena ticket turn-in vs bishop cage key.

options.A: Tickets: OP_25 check then separate OP_28 consume in the same script step.
options.B: Tickets: handled inside OP_0E arena selectors (engine inventory logic); keys: explicit OP_28 in castle scripts.
options.C: Both tickets and keys use only OP_25; OP_28 is for quest discs/rings.
options.D: Keys check without consume — you keep the key after freeing a bishop.

questions.re_guess_detail: OP_28 consume hits map to keys/discs/rings; no ticket item IDs (208–211) appear in OP_28 contexts; arena tiles call exec_selector instead (Partial).
questions.example: Arena tiles use exec_selector (no ticket IDs in script). Bishop keys: loc 55 Red Key 113, loc 57 Yellow Key 112, loc 58 Black Key 114 via OP_28 consume.

questions.title: Gold predicate (OP_24)
questions.scenario: "Travel for 25 gold (y/n)?" — you accept and have enough gold.

options.A: OP_24 is check only (gold ≥ amount); a later op/engine call deducts if you proceed.
options.B: OP_24 both checks and subtracts gold immediately when true.
options.C: OP_24 checks individual member gold, not party total.
options.D: Failed check still deducts partial gold.

questions.re_guess_detail: Scripts pair check_gold_at_least(N) with if not cond: skip then map_transition / party_effect; no subtract in the OP_24 handler (Partial).
questions.example: Atlantium loc 1 tile (2,6): check_gold_at_least(25) → "Not enough gold!" or map_transition. Same pattern for Otto 10gp, Olympic 500gp, Sandsobar trader 200gp.

questions.title: Combat-gated token skip (OP_2B)
questions.scenario: When does the skip walk actually run? (A4-$77BD must be set.)

options.A: Primarily after combat victory / arena return cleanup (flag set @ 0x12438 and related combat paths).
options.B: Any time you skip a dialog with ESC.
options.C: Whenever a y/n prompt appears.
options.D: You have noticed OP_2B branches firing outside combat — e.g. shops, travel.

questions.re_guess_detail: $77BD is set on victory (combat_victory_rewards) and cleared on many map/event exits; OP_2B no-ops when clear (Partial).
questions.example: Arena-town tiles loc 0/1/3/4: skip_tokens(1) → exec_selector tiered post-arena selectors (0x26–0x29, 0x4A–0x4F). Not used for ordinary shop y/n.

questions.title: Calendar / day gate (OP_23)
questions.scenario: NPCs or tiles that only work on certain days or moon phases.

options.A: Yes — obvious day-locked quests you can name (matches OP_23 / day-of-year table).
options.B: Only subtle — benefits "once per moon phase" feel calendar-driven but may use vars, not OP_23.
options.C: Era gates (OP_22) cover all time travel; OP_23 never matters in normal play.
options.D: Unsure — never noticed any day-specific behavior.

questions.re_guess_detail: Moon-phase shrine text uses load_var8 + store_var8, not OP_23; OP_23 reads -$79DE[era] (Partial).
questions.example: loc 8 "Ground Zero!" sign: check_day_of_year day 93 only; loc 10 B2 circus portal: days 140–170 then exec_selector(0x64). Moon shrines use vars, not OP_23.

questions.title: Location 60 — Nordon intro bank
questions.scenario: Corak prologue / Nordon / Nordonna dialogue.

options.A: Shown only during the opening castle/tutorial sequence — not re-triggerable from a town tile later.
options.B: Re-accessible anytime by returning to Middlegate castle tiles.
options.C: Random encounter text anywhere in the world.
options.D: Visible only in the Hall of Spells UI.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 60 is a string_bank; most event segments are missing/trigger-only placeholders, not normal map scripts (Partial).
questions.example: loc 60 strings: Corak prologue, Nordon "Will you do me a service (y/n)?", Nordonna goblet/rescue lines — castle opening bank, not a walkable town tile.

questions.title: Location 61 — spell / hireling tables
questions.scenario: % / letter garbage vs normal prose.

options.A: Never seen in gameplay — metadata for shop/HoS/hire UI only.
options.B: Shows as garbled text if you walk the wrong tile in a town.
options.C: Appears during spell purchase windows as formatting codes.
options.D: Same strings as location 70, duplicated for testing.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 61 decodes as encoded tables; no readable quest prose in the string list (Partial).
questions.example: loc 61 string_bank: "%%%%%%%%", "%%%%9999", ",,,,====", "YYYYZZZZ" — bytecode fragments mixed in, not walk-up quest prose.

questions.title: Location 67 — Hall of Spells mixed pool
questions.scenario: Spell purchase / class check scripts vs dead data.

options.A: Dead — superseded entirely by loc 70 strings.
options.B: Still executed via queued dispatch (0x176B6) for HoS/class flows; contains opcodes outside 0x00–0x32.
options.C: Only runs during endgame (OP_22 era gate).
options.D: You have bought spells from a tile whose script clearly lives in loc 67.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 67 has runnable segments (prompt_yes_no + map_transition) plus non-standard opcodes; overlay path differs from tile scanner (Partial).
questions.example: loc 67 mixed_pool: seg 1 prompt_yes_no → map_transition; seg 8 check_monster_present + consume_item(N-19 Capitor). Non-standard opcodes outside 0x00–0x32.

questions.title: Location 70 — meta bank (bishops, puzzles, HoS)
questions.scenario: Bishop cage text, "Yellow Interleave" puzzle, Triple Crown hints.

options.A: Pulled during castle/bishop events; all four bishops use the same key-check/consume pattern (color-matched key).
options.B: Bishops use tickets; keys are Sandsobar-only.
options.C: Puzzle text is shown as a normal OP_06 sign in Pinehurst overworld only.
options.D: Loc 70 is never read at runtime — editor artifact.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 70 strings include Green Bishop + Yellow Interleave; castle overlays consume Yellow/Red/Black keys via OP_28 matching bishop color (Partial).
questions.example: loc 70: "Yellow Interleave" puzzle, "Bishop of Green Battle is locked in a cage", freed-by-green-key text. Runtime OP_28 on loc 55/57/58 with color keys.

questions.title: Castle blobs (locations 63 / 65 / 68)
questions.scenario: Runtime behavior vs opaque static data.

options.A: Normal tile events fire from these records today (bishop cages, sieges).
options.B: Partial — engine reads blob bytes directly; not via standard triplet scanner (00 00 00 missing).
options.C: Completely unused on Amiga retail — dead padding.
options.D: Only used if you hack event.dat — vanilla never touches them.

questions.re_guess_detail: Flagged castle_blob in inventory; queued dispatch likely consumes bytes without normal init (Partial).
questions.example: loc 63/65/68: castle_blob, no 00 00 00 terminator, hundreds of triplets, 0 script bytes, 0 strings — engine reads blob directly.

questions.title: Olympic Trial (Atlantium, event @ "Hurl the spear…")
questions.scenario: Text-only today — ever combat later?

options.A: Permanently non-combat — pays 500 gp to teach Athlete skill via apply_party / party_effect.
options.B: Starts combat if you refuse the y/n prompt.
options.C: Becomes a combat arena after a quest flag or era change.
options.D: Was combat in an earlier retail build — patch changed it.

questions.re_guess_detail: loc 01 event 27 decompiles to skill purchase, not encounter_setup; user already reported text-only (Partial).
questions.example: loc 1 Atlantium tile (4,8) "The Olympic Trial": 500gp y/n → apply_party_masked Athlete bit → party_effect — no encounter_setup anywhere.

questions.title: Arena ticket color per town
questions.scenario: Which colored ticket item gates each town arena? (items.dat: Green 208, Yellow 209, Red 210, Black 211 — note typo "Yellow Tickt".)

options.A: Middlegate=Green, Atlantium=Yellow, Sandsobar=Black, Vulcania=Red.
options.B: Middlegate=Yellow, Atlantium=Green, Sandsobar=Red, Vulcania=Black.
options.C: All four towns accept any ticket color.
options.D: Tundara sells tickets but has no arena — ignore for pairing.

questions.re_guess_detail: Vulcania=Red is user-verified; four ticket items match four arena towns; Sandsobar Triple Crown text highlights Black ticket for cross-arena quest (Partial — exact shop→color mapping not in decoded scripts).
questions.example: items 208–211 Green/Yellow/Red/Black tickets. loc 57 Pinehurst Triple Crown: "take a Black Ticket to the Arena, Monster Bowl, and Colosseum". Arena entry uses exec_selector per town, not ticket IDs in script.
